title: Functions of a Complex Variable (3CV)
outline: This course introduces students to the calculus of functions of one complex variable, with special focus on applications to other disciplines (e.g. Physics). Topics include: calculus of complex functions of one variable; conformal maps; complex contour integration and the Cauchy-Goursat theorem; Taylor and Laurent series of complex functions; calculus of residues; applications of complex contour integration to real-valued integrals; principal value of an integral; multi-valued complex functions; analytic continuation; and special functions.
dp_requirements: A class record of 30% or more; satisfactory attendance at lectures and tutorials; submission of all assigned work.
assessment: Class record counts up to 50%; the balance is made up by the final examination.
